question_text: Jupiter has a diameter of approximately 143,000 km, while Earth has a diameter of approximately 12,700 km. Which statement best describes the size comparison?
options: Jupiter is about 5 times wider than Earth
Jupiter is about 11 times wider than Earth
Jupiter is about 20 times wider than Earth
Jupiter is about 50 times wider than Earth
correct_answer: Jupiter is about 11 times wider than Earth
explanation: To compare sizes, you divide Jupiter's diameter by Earth's diameter: 143,000 ÷ 12,700 ≈ 11. This means Jupiter is roughly 11 times wider across than Earth, showing why it is the largest planet in our solar system.
